action.reasoning: Observation: the greedy soup procedure selected the existing 9-member uniform ConvNeXt soup and hidden accuracy stayed at the best 0.7776, indicating validation did not support adding any extra individual checkpoint. Analysis: weight-space averaging is the only post-hoc method that improved hidden performance, and with two iterations left a low-cost exploration is still possible. The eight extra ConvNeXt checkpoints were harmful in logit space but may act differently in weight space; a simple non-validation-tuned all-17 uniform soup tests whether broader basin averaging improves hidden generalization. Decision: create a uniform ConvNeXt soup from all compatible individual ConvNeXt checkpoints (excluding prior soup checkpoints/ensembles) and submit it. If it underperforms, final iteration will resubmit the known-best convnext_soup9_uniform.pt.
